summary: ITER9 web-push + P0 PresenceDot import fix — ALL OBJECTIVES VERIFIED. Backend: 13/13 new push tests PASS + full regression 117/117 PASS (~40s). Frontend: chat page loads with zero console/page errors (P0 PresenceDot crash resolved), Profile page exposes toggle-webpush-button + webpush-status-dot + webpush-status-label ('not subscribed'), /sw.js served 200 application/javascript with push + notificationclick handlers, admin console works, message send works, ChannelSidebar DM list renders with presence dots. VAPID key persistence verified across `sudo supervisorctl restart backend` (identical 87-char base64url key before & after).
critical_code_review_comments: push_service.py init_vapid_keys correctly idempotent: reads singleton {key: 'vapid_keys'} first, only generates + upserts on cold DB. Public key is Base64URL(UncompressedPoint of EC P-256) => 65 raw bytes => 87 chars without padding, starting with 'B' (0x04 leading byte encodes to 'B' in base64url) — verified live.

routes/push.py /subscribe uses `update_one(filter=endpoint, $set=..., upsert=True)` keyed ONLY on endpoint. This means if user A subscribes on device X and later user B somehow posts the same endpoint, it would be reassigned. In practice each push-service endpoint is device-scoped so collisions are near-zero, but an optional hardening is to filter by {endpoint, user_id} in the upsert selector and let a second user_id create a separate doc. Current design is fine for this scale.
routes/push.py /unsubscribe correctly scopes to user_id so user A cannot drop user B's subscription by endpoint guess. Good.

push_service._send_one swallows WebPushException + generic Exception and logs — this is what keeps /api/push/test from 500-ing when the FCM endpoint is bogus. Verified via test_with_fake_sub_does_not_500 (fake FCM endpoint registered, /push/test still returns 200).
push_service.send_to_user auto-cleans 404/410 (gone) subscriptions — good housekeeping. Other non-fatal statuses (e.g., 413 payload too large) are left in DB which is correct.

ProfilePage.jsx renders `test-webpush-button` ONLY when subscribed=true (line 254-263). This is correct UX (can't test without a subscription) — automated Chromium cannot actually subscribe (no real push service / user gesture for Notification.requestPermission in headless) so this button is expected to be absent in tests. Review request explicitly says 'Do NOT attempt to test real web-push delivery'. Not a bug.

public/sw.js is minimal and correct: handles 'push' (falls back to .text() if JSON parse fails), 'notificationclick' (focuses existing same-origin tab + postMessage 'push:click', else openWindow). skipWaiting + clients.claim on install/activate so updates take effect immediately — standard CRA pattern.

MessageList.jsx line 4 adds `import PresenceDot from './PresenceDot'` — P0 fix verified: chat page loads with 0 page errors, 0 console errors, message-list renders, MessageRow shows presence dot under avatar (see iter9_chat.png screenshot).
test_credentials: Admin: operations@panoramacoaches.com.au / Pano3666 — unchanged, NOT modified during testing.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed. Each test cleans up after itself: subscribes with a single fake FCM endpoint 'https://fcm.googleapis.com/fcm/send/TEST_ITER9_FAKE_ENDPOINT_PANORAMA' and unsubscribes in teardown. One transient public-channel message 'TEST_iter9 push _fire_push safety probe' is posted + deleted in test_send_message_in_public_channel_does_not_500_with_fake_sub. Frontend probe also posted 'TEST_iter9 frontend probe' via composer (not cleaned — low impact, single admin message in #general).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Web-push feature is production-ready from an API surface perspective. Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_iterN_full.xml` for full regression (~40s, 117 tests). Push suite: test_iter9_push.py covers VAPID key shape+persistence, /push/subscribe auth+validation+upsert, /push/unsubscribe auth+scope, /push/test safety with and without subs, _fire_push safety on message POST, and /sw.js reachability+content-type. To retest VAPID persistence manually: curl $BACKEND/api/push/vapid-public-key, `sudo supervisorctl restart backend && sleep 5`, curl again — keys must match (verified this iter). REAL push delivery to a browser cannot be automated (needs real FCM/Mozilla push + OS notification surface + user gesture for Notification.requestPermission); all we can do is verify our API accepts/rejects payloads correctly and pywebpush is invoked without throwing. test-webpush-button is conditionally rendered only after `subscribed=true`, which requires a real navigator.serviceWorker.register + PushManager.subscribe — not reproducible in headless automation; this is expected, not a bug.
rca of the issue: N/A — this iteration is a feature implementation (web-push) + one P0 fix (missing PresenceDot import). Both verified working. P0 RCA for reference: MessageList.jsx referenced <PresenceDot/> JSX without importing the component, causing 'PresenceDot is not defined' ReferenceError and React error boundary crash on chat page mount. Fix: add `import PresenceDot from './PresenceDot'` at top of MessageList.jsx. Verified: 0 console errors, 0 page errors, message-list + presence dots render.
